objects.title: Application Object
objects.purpose: The root runtime object of Premiere Pro automation.
constraints: Identity MUST NOT be inferred from display name.
Unknown fields MUST survive serialization.
ai_rules: Determine object scope before editing.
Do not fabricate unsupported API methods.

objects.title: Project Object
objects.purpose: The open project containing bins, items and sequences.
constraints: Identity MUST NOT be inferred from display name.
Unknown fields MUST survive serialization.
ai_rules: Determine object scope before editing.
Do not fabricate unsupported API methods.

objects.title: ProjectItem Object
objects.purpose: A project-level reference to source media, sequence, bin, or generated asset.
constraints: Identity MUST NOT be inferred from display name.
Unknown fields MUST survive serialization.
ai_rules: Determine object scope before editing.
Do not fabricate unsupported API methods.

objects.title: Sequence Object
objects.purpose: A timeline container that owns tracks and markers but not media files.
constraints: Identity MUST NOT be inferred from display name.
Unknown fields MUST survive serialization.
ai_rules: Determine object scope before editing.
Do not fabricate unsupported API methods.

objects.title: Track Object
objects.purpose: A video, audio, or caption lane that owns timeline items.
constraints: Identity MUST NOT be inferred from display name.
Unknown fields MUST survive serialization.
ai_rules: Determine object scope before editing.
Do not fabricate unsupported API methods.

objects.title: TrackItem Object
objects.purpose: A timeline instance referencing a ProjectItem and owning components.
constraints: Identity MUST NOT be inferred from display name.
Unknown fields MUST survive serialization.
ai_rules: Determine object scope before editing.
Do not fabricate unsupported API methods.

objects.title: Component Object
objects.purpose: An effect or intrinsic module exposing editable properties.
constraints: Identity MUST NOT be inferred from display name.
Unknown fields MUST survive serialization.
ai_rules: Determine object scope before editing.
Do not fabricate unsupported API methods.

objects.title: Property Object
objects.purpose: A typed editable value exposed by a component.
constraints: Identity MUST NOT be inferred from display name.
Unknown fields MUST survive serialization.
ai_rules: Determine object scope before editing.
Do not fabricate unsupported API methods.

objects.title: Caption Object
objects.purpose: A timed text unit with text, formatting and metadata.
constraints: Identity MUST NOT be inferred from display name.
Unknown fields MUST survive serialization.
ai_rules: Determine object scope before editing.
Do not fabricate unsupported API methods.

objects.title: Marker Object
objects.purpose: A timed annotation owned by project, sequence or clip scope.
constraints: Identity MUST NOT be inferred from display name.
Unknown fields MUST survive serialization.
ai_rules: Determine object scope before editing.
Do not fabricate unsupported API methods.

objects.title: Bin Object
objects.purpose: Define the Bin entity in the Premiere knowledge model for AI reasoning and automation planning.
objects.summary: Bin is modeled as a distinct Premiere knowledge entity. AI must not collapse it into adjacent concepts.
constraints: Object identity must not be inferred from display name alone.
AI must distinguish source-level objects from timeline-instance objects.
validation: Check whether operation changes source object, timeline instance, or serialized export.

api.title: CEP Boundary
api.purpose: Define the boundary, risks and planning rules for CEP Boundary.
api.summary: CEP panels can host HTML/JS UI and call ExtendScript. CEP remains common in installed tooling but should be treated as legacy when planning new long-lived extensions.
constraints: Do not mix APIs in one solution unless the bridging mechanism is explicit.
Always state whether a proposed method is ExtendScript, UXP, CEP, QE, Python, XML, or external automation.
ai_rules: If the API surface is unknown, stop and ask for verification instead of inventing methods.

api.title: Encoder Boundary
api.purpose: Define the boundary, risks and planning rules for Encoder Boundary.
api.summary: Encoding workflows may use Premiere export APIs or Adobe Media Encoder integration. Export plans must specify preset, path, side effects and queue behavior.
constraints: Do not mix APIs in one solution unless the bridging mechanism is explicit.
Always state whether a proposed method is ExtendScript, UXP, CEP, QE, Python, XML, or external automation.
ai_rules: If the API surface is unknown, stop and ask for verification instead of inventing methods.

api.title: ExtendScript Boundary
api.purpose: Define the boundary, risks and planning rules for ExtendScript Boundary.
api.summary: ExtendScript is the legacy scripting surface used by CEP panels and scripts. It is synchronous and remains important for existing integrations, but new work should evaluate UXP first.
constraints: Do not mix APIs in one solution unless the bridging mechanism is explicit.
Always state whether a proposed method is ExtendScript, UXP, CEP, QE, Python, XML, or external automation.
ai_rules: If the API surface is unknown, stop and ask for verification instead of inventing methods.

api.title: QE Boundary
api.purpose: Define the boundary, risks and planning rules for QE Boundary.
api.summary: QE is an unofficial/internal interface used by many scripts. AI agents must treat QE as unstable and require explicit user consent before suggesting QE-dependent workflows.
constraints: Do not mix APIs in one solution unless the bridging mechanism is explicit.
Always state whether a proposed method is ExtendScript, UXP, CEP, QE, Python, XML, or external automation.
ai_rules: If the API surface is unknown, stop and ask for verification instead of inventing methods.

api.title: UXP Boundary
api.purpose: Define the boundary, risks and planning rules for UXP Boundary.
api.summary: UXP is the modern plugin and scripting platform for Premiere Pro. UXP exposes the Premiere DOM through require("premierepro"); Premiere operations may require asynchronous calls.
constraints: Do not mix APIs in one solution unless the bridging mechanism is explicit.
Always state whether a proposed method is ExtendScript, UXP, CEP, QE, Python, XML, or external automation.
ai_rules: If the API surface is unknown, stop and ask for verification instead of inventing methods.

api.title: ExtendScript App API Surface
api.purpose: Capture the ExtendScript/CEP scripting surface related to App.
api.summary: Structured placeholder for method/property inventory, return types, version matrix, examples, pitfalls and failure modes.
constraints: Do not assume methods from After Effects scripting exist in Premiere.
Do not assume UXP methods exist in ExtendScript.
ai_rules: Generated JSX must use only verified ExtendScript methods or explicitly mark unverified areas.

api.title: UXP Application API Surface
api.purpose: Capture the UXP Premiere DOM surface related to Application.
api.summary: Structured placeholder for method/property inventory, version matrix, examples and failure modes. Populate only from official UXP docs, official samples, or reproducible local tests.
constraints: Minimum version must be recorded per member when known.
Async/sync behavior must be recorded per method/property.
validation: No method may be used in generated code unless it appears in this API surface or is confirmed by a cited experiment.

serialization.title: Premiere XML Serialization
serialization.purpose: Defines a safe XML interchange layer for Premiere automation.
constraints: XML export can lose Premiere-internal information.
XML MUST be validated before reimport.
ai_rules: Prefer official API when available.
Use XML as controlled interchange, not as full project truth.

serialization.title: Caption JSON Serialization
serialization.purpose: Defines constraints for caption JSON and text/style run preservation.
constraints: Text run lengths MUST match text content.
Unknown JSON nodes MUST be preserved.
Style ranges MUST remain internally consistent.
ai_rules: Never regenerate full caption JSON from plain text.
Edit the smallest possible node set.
